hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 complementarity, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) affinity by avoiding off-target-conserved electrostatic interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that strong ddG binding alone is insufficient for success; selectivity must be explicitly optimized by disrupting conserved off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. The next iteration will focus on this strategy to increase Δmargin beyond the native SST-14 baseline.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt off-target interactions at conserved positions that are not part of the pharmacophore. Focusing on these positions allows us to design mutations that reduce SSTR3/SSTR5 binding while preserving SSTR2 specificity.

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore region (positions 7-10) or the Cys3/Cys14 disulfide bridge. The proposed residues are expected to reduce off-target binding while maintaining SSTR2 affinity.
